violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2026

Sausage and eggs observed held at 90-118 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution, as the test strips on site expired September 1, 2024. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2026
The receptacle in the employee toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 03/22/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/01/2026

Dishwasher was nonfunctional at time of inspection, with no chemicals hooked up. All equipment and utensils are returned to Smithfield High School to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025
Facility is lacking a 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/01/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2022

Elote corn observed held at 118 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Elote corn was reheated. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
Establishment is lacking a 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/11/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2023

Hummus was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The refrigerator in the dishwasher area was not in good repair, not maintaining food at 41 degrees F or colder. Foods requiring refrigeration are not to be stored in it until it is functioning properly. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
The facility is lacking a 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the can opener base have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2023

Pasta was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024
Turkey was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in a container greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the 2 refrigerators. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024
Facility is lacking a 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/11/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the can opener base have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. The large chest freezer has an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Once food safety certificate is obtained, PIC to register Rhode Island Certified Food Protection Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2024

Taco meat was received at 107 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC called high school to verify time and temperature when taco meat was cooked. Food was reheated to 205 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Hot foods must be received at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or more. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024
